title: Diabetes Mellitus | Clinical Medicine
description:
Exclusive USMLE Step 2/PANCE Lecture… for FREE! Become a member on our website for more Premium Resources: https://ninjanerd.org/lecture/diabetes-mellitus

Ninja Nerds!
In this lecture, Professor Zach Murphy will present on Diabetes Mellitus. We will discuss the pathophysiology, differentiating between Type 1 diabetes, characterized by autoimmune destruction of insulin-producing beta cells in the pancreas, and Type 2 diabetes, resulting from insulin resistance and relative insulin deficiency. The lecture will include a discussion on the clinical manifestations, such as polyuria, polydipsia, and unexplained weight loss. The digital presentation will begin with the diagnostic approach, highlighting the importance of fasting plasma glucose levels, the oral glucose tolerance test, and glycated hemoglobin (HbA1c) measurements. Finally, we will review management strategies for Diabetes Mellitus, emphasizing the role of dietary management, physical activity, blood glucose monitoring, insulin therapy for Type 1 diabetes, and oral hypoglycemic agents for Type 2 diabetes. Enjoy the lecture and support us below!

Table of Contents:
0:00 Lab
0:07 Diabetes Mellitus Introduction
0:18 Pathophysiology of Diabetes Mellitus
8:56 Complications/Issues with Diabetes Mellitus
21:40 Diagnostic Approach to Diabetes Mellitus
25:21 Treatment of Type I Diabetes Mellitus
30:07 Treatment of Type II Diabetes Mellitus
35:23 Treatment of Diabetes Mellitus Complications
44:35 Comment, Like, SUBSCRIBE!

title: How There Could Finally Be A Cure For Diabetes
description:
Over the past 20 years, significant advancements in stem cell research and therapies have been one of the most promising methods of creating new insulin making cells needed to cure type 1 diabetes. 

Vertex Pharmaceuticals, a biotech company, recently began a clinical trial where it plans to treat 17 participants who have type 1 diabetes with new stem cell derived insulin making cells. The first patient in the trial has had positive results. 

Other companies around the world including ViaCyte and CRISPR, as well as Novo Nordisk, one of the biggest insulin manufacturers in the world, are also working on curing the disease. CNBC explores why finding a cure for diabetes is so hard and just how close Vertex and other companies are to solving this problem.

description:

The advice in this video is not intended to replace the instructions from your diabetes team. Please talk to your team about how to tailor this information to meet your needs.

title: Clinical Cuts: Diabetes mellitus
description:
Access to affordable healthcare for folks with diabetes has been a pressing issue in many communities. But how exactly does diabetes affect the body? Today's Clinical Cut is all about diabetes mellitus.

In diabetes mellitus, your body has trouble moving glucose, which is a type of sugar, from your blood into your cells.

This leads to high levels of glucose in your blood and not enough of it in your cells, and remember that your cells need glucose as a source of energy, so not letting the glucose enter means that the cells starve for energy despite having glucose right on their doorstep.

In general, the body controls how much glucose is in the blood relative to how much gets into the cells with two hormones: insulin and glucagon.

Insulin is used to reduce blood glucose levels, and glucagon is used to increase blood glucose levels. Both of these hormones are produced by clusters of cells in the pancreas called islets of Langerhans. Insulin is secreted by beta cells in the center of the islets, and glucagon is secreted by alpha cells in the periphery of the islets.

There are two types of diabetes - Type 1 and Type 2, and the main difference between them is the underlying mechanism that causes the blood glucose levels to rise.

Diabetes mellitus is diagnosed when the blood glucose levels get too high, and this is seen among 10% of the world population. About 10% of people with diabetes have Type 1, and the remaining 90% of people with diabetes have Type 2.

Check out the full video and explanation to learn more about diabetes mellitus on Osmosis: https://osms.it/cc-diabetes-yts

title: Diabetes 101
description:
Meet Eric and Tami, two friends who recently found out they share a challenging health diagnosis. They both have diabetes. Diabetes is a disease that occurs when the amount of glucose or blood sugar in the body gets too high. It can be dangerous if untreated because high blood sugar impacts our ability to function daily and can lead to serious health problems such as heart disease, vision loss, amputations, kidney disease, and even death, if not properly managed.

To learn more visit:

https://www.fda.gov/HealthEquity

title: Doctor Explains 13 Skin Signs of Diabetes - Never Ignore These Symptoms (High Sugar)
description:
In this video we look a 13 Skin signs linked with diabetes and high sugar levels: 
Other Helpful videos - 7 foods to avoid if you have diabetes 👉🏼👉🏼👉🏼 https://youtu.be/wL1TxsKKZ7A
Signs Diabetes and Pre-diabetes that MOST people don't know about: 👉🏼👉🏼👉🏼 youtu.be/2svdADrGwy8

1) Necrobiosis Lipoidica
This is called Necrobiosis Lipoidica - it's an uncommon inflammatory condition that usually 
The condition is most commonly seen in people with diabetes (both type 1 or type 2). 
We don’t really know the cause, some theories suggest it could be damage to areas 
that give the skin its strength - your collagen fibers. 
Some think this is due to changes in the small blood vessels of the skin.
https://www.thelancet.com/cms/attachment/61e43891-a157-4f19-8488-b52d72cb2d30/gr1.jpg

2)Dark Skin Patches (Acanthosis Negricans)
The reason why it happens isn’t clear cut but it is thought to be linked with insulin resistance.
This is why it can occur if we are overweight or obese. But is also linked with diabetes and some metabolic disorders - like Polycystic ovary syndrome and Cushing’s syndrome.
https://www.nhs.uk/conditions/acanthosis-nigricans/

3) Granuloma Annulare
It is thought that the process behind why it appears is most likely due to inflammation. It can be linked with a few conditions, including - diabetes, autoimmune thyroiditis, hyperlipidemia and very rarely, some forms of cancer. 
https://www.nhs.uk/conditions/granuloma-annulare/

4) Xanthelasma
This happens when your body collects extra cholesterol around your eyes. 

https://www.myheart.org.sg/heart-news/xanthelasma-a-sign-of-high-blood-cholesterol/
https://clinicafai.com/understanding-xanthelasma-the-weird-yellow-deposits-on-your-eyelids/

5) Eruptive Xanthomatosis)
If you develop a break out of small reddish-yellow bumps across your body - this CAN BE a sign of underlying metabolic disease. The skin eruptions are usually non-tender but in some cases cause may be itchy, but the good news is they’re not contagious.

https://www.healthline.com/health/eruptive-xanthomatosis#outlook

6) Diabetic cheiroarthropathy
Its characterized by waxy skin-thickening over the palm of the hand which restricts mobility, particularly affecting the extension of the MCPs and PIP joints

7) Foot Ulcers
Diabetics can have poor circulation and high sugar levels, which can slow down the healing process and make you more prone to infections. Getting wounds infected can prolong the amount of time it takes to heal. 

8) Skin tags
Some people can get these as a result of diabetes. The reason isn’t fully understood but its likely a number of factors including insulin resistance, obesity, and hormonal changes
https://www.healthdirect.gov.au/skin-tags

9) Diabetic Dermopathy
Diabetic dermopathy is it's thought to be related to damage to the small blood vessels that supply the skin. Poor blood circulation and changes in the structure of the skin may contribute to the development of these patches.

https://diabetes.co.in/what-is-diabetes-dermopathy/

10) Dry itchy skin
But itchy skin can result from dry skin or poor circulation, both of which are more likely when you have diabetes. 

11) Fungal infection
When we are diabetic and have high sugar levels - this can encourage the growth of fungal infections. 

12)Bacteria infection
Similar to fungal infections - bacterial infections are also more common in diabetics. Other skin infections like impetigo and folloculitis are also more common.

13) Blisters
They can develop if there has been some friction to the skin and due to neuropathy you may not have felt the discomfort 

#diabetessymptoms #diabetes #prediabetes

title: Intermittent Fasting for Type 2 Diabetes (Step by Step) | Jason Fung
description:
Intermittent Fasting for Type 2 Diabetes (Step by Step) | Jason Fung

🔄 Navigating Type 2 Diabetes: A Step-by-Step Guide to Intermittent Fasting ⌛
Embark on a transformative journey as we explore the powerful relationship between intermittent fasting and managing Type 2 Diabetes. Key insights include:

🤔 What is Type 2 Diabetes?: Gain a comprehensive understanding of Type 2 Diabetes and its impact on the body.
🔄 De Novo Lipogenesis: Explore the process of de novo lipogenesis, shedding light on how the body converts excess sugars into fat.
💨 Gas Analogy: Uncover the gas analogy, providing a clear illustration of how intermittent fasting can influence insulin resistance and glucose regulation.
🤷‍♂️ Does Fasting Work?: Delve into the scientific evidence supporting the efficacy of fasting as a powerful tool in managing Type 2 Diabetes.
🌟 How to Do Fasting?: Receive a step-by-step guide on incorporating intermittent fasting into your routine, tailored specifically for managing Type 2 Diabetes.
🤝 Conclusion: Summarize the key takeaways and insights, emphasizing the potential benefits of intermittent fasting for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes.

Join us in unraveling the intricacies of Type 2 Diabetes management through step-by-step guidance on incorporating intermittent fasting. Hit the like button 👍, subscribe for more empowering content, and ring the notification bell 🔔 to stay informed!

title: How to STOP Prediabetes from Turning into Diabetes
description:
Get access to my FREE resources 👉 https://drbrg.co/45aulSQ

Here’s how to stop prediabetes going into diabetes. This is important. 

NEW KETO RECIPES CHANNEL: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCnAGH-jp4YXl30rir1BPrxA/

For more details on this topic, check out the full article on the website:
➡️ https://drbrg.co/480ddj6

Healthy Ketogenic Diet and Intermittent Fasting:
▶️ https://youtu.be/vMZfyEy_jpI
▶️ https://youtu.be/mBqpaAKtnXE
▶️ https://youtu.be/3dHcT1-K-tw 

Timestamps
0:00 Stop prediabetes going into diabetes 
0:25 The difference between prediabetes and diabetes
1:30 A closer look at blood sugar and insulin 
3:15 Insulin resistance 
5:00 What you could do 
5:25 The test you need that’s never done 
6:13 Symptoms of insulin resistance 
8:19 Other problems with sugar 

Today we’re going to talk about how to help stop a person from going from a prediabetic state to a diabetic state. This is a very important concept. 

The difference between prediabetes and diabetes has to do with your blood sugars. 

 • With prediabetes, your blood sugars are around 100-125, with an A1C of 5.7-6.4%. 
 • With diabetes, your blood sugars are 126 and greater, with an A1C of 6.5% or greater. 

When we’re talking about diabetes type 2, we’re talking about the elevation of sugar in your blood. A normal blood sugar level should be about 80. This is equal to about 1 tsp of sugar in all of your blood. Your body can make this sugar itself. You don’t need to consume any sugar. The average person consumes about 31 tsp of sugar every day (including carbs).

Insulin acts as a filter that keeps excess sugar out of the blood. The body does not want high amounts of sugar in the blood. Your body considers excess sugar to be very dangerous. 

If a person is consuming a lot of carbohydrates and eats frequently, they could have a huge amount of insulin being released. This insulin is working very hard to keep the sugar down. But, high levels of insulin are toxic to the body as well. So, your body starts to reduce insulin by creating resistance. This is called insulin resistance. 

The problem is that the body still has to keep the blood sugar under control, so it begins to produce more and more insulin to try to keep the sugar at a normal level. This could go on for a long time until, eventually, the pancreas is exhausted and can’t pump out that much insulin. 

At this point, if you keep consuming carbohydrates and eating frequently, the blood sugar is going to go up. This happens because the insulin is going down. Over time this could turn into diabetes. 

To help avoid all of this, you can cut out the carbs and start doing intermittent fasting. 

Doctors are focused on blood sugars. They typically do not measure if you have insulin resistance. 90% of people that are prediabetic don’t even know. 

If you look down and you can’t see your feet, you may have insulin resistance. 

Symptoms of insulin resistance:
 • Frequent urination (even at night) 
 • Brain fog
 • Can't go long without being hungry 
 • Cravings 
 • Tired after meals 
 • Tingling feet

title: Why is diabetes spreading around the world? | Inside Story
description:
The number of adults living with diabetes worldwide will more than double by 2050 -- surpassing most diseases on a global scale.
That’s according to a study to published in the Lancet medical journal.
The research reveals more than half a billion people currently live with diabetes worldwide, and every country is expected to see a major increase.
Rapidly rising levels of obesity and widening inequalities in healthcare are identified as key factors.
Will the world heed the warning and address the diabetes threat? 
And can a healthier future be secured for everyone?

Presenter: Folly Bah Thibault

Guests
Dr. Rayaz Malik - Professor of Medicine at Weill Cornell Medicine in Qatar and a pioneering researcher of diabetes.
Dr. Shivani Agarwal - Associate Professor of Medicine at the Albert Einstein College of Medicine. Lead author on one of the Lancet reports.
Mohammad Dabbah - Head of Artifical Intelligence at sports data provider Statsbomb.

@AljazeeraEnglish

title: 12 Unusual Symptoms of Diabetes | Diabetes Symptoms | Diabetes Mellitus |  | Diabetes Warning Signs
description:
12 Unusual Symptoms of Diabetes | Diabetes Symptoms | Diabetes Mellitus |  | Diabetes Warning Signs

In this video we have discussed some early and unusual signs and symptoms of diabetes.
There are mainly 3 types of diabetes:
Type – I: where insulin is completely absent
Type – II: where either insulin formation decreases, or the body is not responding to the insulin formed.
Gestational diabetes - Gestational diabetes develops in pregnant women who have never had diabetes

How can you tell if you have diabetes? 
Most early symptoms are from higher-than-normal levels of glucose.

What are the early signs of Diabetes?

Hunger and Fatigue
The food we eat is converted into glucose, which our cells use for energy. Our cells need insulin to take in glucose. But in diabetes there isn’t enough or any insulin. So the glucose can't get into them and you have no energy. This can make you hungrier and more tired than usual.

Peeing more often and being thirstier
A person with diabetes may need to pee a lot more, than an average person.

Dry mouth & Itchy Skin
Because the body is using a lot of fluid to make urine, there's less moisture available for other things. This can make you dehydrated, and your Mouth may feel dry. 

Blurred vision
Changing fluid levels in body can make the lens in Eyes swell up. 

What are the symptoms of Diabetes
These appear after the glucose level is high for a long time.

Fungal or yeast infections:
Excess glucose in your body promotes fungal infection. In diabetes there can be fungal infections
Between fingers and toes
Under breasts
In or around genital organs.

Slow-healing sores or cuts
Over time, high blood sugar can affect the blood flow and it may even cause nerve damage. 
That makes it hard for your body to heal wounds.

Pain or numbness in feet or legs
This is also a result of nerve damage caused by high blood glucose.

Unplanned weight loss
If your body can't get energy from the food you consume, then it will start burning muscle and fat for energy.  This leads to the unintentional weight loss.

Nausea & Vomiting
When the body burns fat, it makes ketones. These ketones can build up in blood to dangerous levels. These Ketones can make you feel sick.

What are the warning Signs of Diabetes Complications
Signs of diabetes' complications may include:
Slow-healing sores or cuts
Itchy skin (usually around the vaginal or groin area)
Frequent yeast infections.
Recent weight gain.
Velvety, dark skin changes of the neck, armpit, and groin, called Acanthosis nigricans
Numbness and tingling of the hands and feet
Decreased vision
Impotence or erectile dysfunction (ED)

A serious complication of prolonged increase in blood glucose is Diabetic Coma
This serious complication can even lead to death. 
It may occur with either type of diabetes, though it’s more common in type 2. 

Other complications of diabetes are:
Heart disease and stroke
Diabetic retinopathy
Glaucoma
Cataract
Chronic kidney disease (CKD)
Neuropathy
Gum diseases

When to visit a Doctor
If you're older than 45 or have other risks for diabetes, it's important to get tested. 
When you diagnose the condition early, you can avoid nerve damage, Heart trouble, and other complications.

title: Gestational Diabetes: Everything You Need to Know
description:
.

Chapters

0:09 Introduction
1:07 Causes of Gestational Diabetes
2:49 Diagnosis and treatment
4:07 Treatment




Gestational diabetes is a condition in which a woman without diabetes develops high blood sugar levels during pregnancy.[2] Gestational diabetes generally results in few symptoms;[2] however, it does increase the risk of pre-eclampsia, depression, and requiring a Caesarean section.[2] Babies born to mothers with poorly treated gestational diabetes are at increased risk of being too large, having low blood sugar after birth, and jaundice.[2] If untreated, it can also result in a stillbirth.[2] Long term, children are at higher risk of being overweight and developing type 2 diabetes.[2]

Gestational diabetes can occur during pregnancy because of insulin resistance or reduced production of insulin.[2] Risk factors include being overweight, previously having gestational diabetes, a family history of type 2 diabetes, and having polycystic ovarian syndrome.[2] Diagnosis is by blood tests.[2] For those at normal risk, screening is recommended between 24 and 28 weeks' gestation.[2][3] For those at high risk, testing may occur at the first prenatal visit.[2]

Prevention is by maintaining a healthy weight and exercising before pregnancy.[2] Gestational diabetes is treated with a diabetic diet, exercise, medication (such as metformin), and possibly insulin injections.[2] Most women are able to manage their blood sugar with diet and exercise.[3] Blood sugar testing among those who are affected is often recommended four times a day.[3] Breastfeeding is recommended as soon as possible after birth.[2]

Gestational diabetes affects 3–9% of pregnancies, depending on the population studied.[3] It is especially common during the last three months of pregnancy.[2] It affects 1% of those under the age of 20 and 13% of those over the age of 44.[3] A number of ethnic groups including Asians, American Indians, Indigenous Australians, and Pacific Islanders are at higher risk.[3][2] In 90% of cases, gestational diabetes will resolve after the baby is born.[2] Women, however, are at an increased risk of developing type 2 diabetes.[3]

title: 5 Early Signs of Diabetes | Diabetes Symptoms | Diabetes Mellitus #shortsvideo#health #diabetes
description:
5 Early Signs of Diabetes | Diabetes Symptoms | Diabetes Mellitus | Diabetes management | Signs of diabetes | Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus | Diabetes symptoms in men | Diabetes symptoms in women | Preventing diabetes | How to recognize early signs of diabetes

5 Early Signs That You Might Have Diabetes
Diabetes is a chronic condition that can have serious health implications if not detected and managed early. Recognizing the early signs of diabetes is crucial for seeking timely treatment. Here are the top 5 early signs that you might have diabetes:

1. Increased Thirst and Urination

One of the most common early signs of diabetes is increased thirst and frequent urination. High blood sugar levels cause your kidneys to work overtime to filter and absorb the excess sugar. This leads to more frequent urination, which can dehydrate your body and make you feel constantly thirsty.

2. Unexplained Weight Loss

If you’re losing weight without making any changes to your diet or exercise routine, it could be a sign of diabetes. When your body doesn’t get enough energy from glucose, it starts to burn fat and muscle for fuel, leading to unexpected weight loss.

3. Fatigue

Feeling unusually tired or fatigued all the time? High blood sugar levels can affect your body’s ability to use glucose for energy, leaving you feeling sluggish and fatigued. This constant tiredness can be a key indicator of diabetes.

4. Blurred Vision

High blood sugar can cause the lenses in your eyes to swell, leading to blurred vision. If you’re experiencing difficulty seeing clearly, it’s important to get your blood sugar levels checked as this can be an early sign of diabetes.

5. Slow-Healing Wounds

Cuts, bruises, and infections that take longer to heal can be a sign of high blood sugar. Diabetes can impair your body’s ability to heal wounds properly, making you more susceptible to infections and slow recovery times.

Conclusion
If you notice any of these early signs, it’s important to consult a healthcare professional for a proper diagnosis. Early detection and management of diabetes can significantly improve your quality of life and prevent serious complications.

title: Nutritionist Cooks Diabetes Friendly Recipes
description:
Cooking meals when you have diabetes can be challenging, but it’s not impossible! Cooking and eating balanced meals is highly recommended to manage your blood sugar. Nutritionist Jo Sebastian give us a rundown of the types of diabetes, what you can be doing differently once you’re diagnosed, and shares two simple recipes for your next meal prep session at home.

Munggo
Ingredients
3 tbsp oil
2 pc tilapia
3 cloves garlic, minced
1 pc red onions, sliced
2 pc tomatoes, sliced
1 cup munggo beans
1 cup malunggay leaves

Procedure:
In a pan with hot oil, cook the fish for 2-3 minutes on each side. Set aside and shred.
Using the same pan, saute garlic and onions followed by the tomatoes.
Add your munggo beans and fill with 4 cups of water. Bring to a boil and simmer for about 40 minutes or until the beans are mashable. Add your malunggay and cook for an additional 2 minutes.

Makes 6 servings

Egg Wrap
Ingredients:
3 pc eggs
1 pc cucumbers, cut in half and sliced
3 pcs tomatoes, sliced
kesong puti
Greek-style yogurt

Procedure:
Beat all eggs in a bowl. On a non-stick skillet, add oil and eggs.
Once the egg is set, loosen the sides and flip. Cook for an additional 30 seconds and set aside.
Assemble by placing the veggies and cheese at the bottom of the egg. Drizzle with yogurt and roll into a wrap.

title: Doctor Teaches Kids About Diabetes
description:
I teamed up with The McClure Twins, Ava and Alexis, to play my version of Mad Libs I call “Med Libs”. They provided answers to all the prompts I threw at them, then I worked those words into a story educating them about Type 1 Diabetes. We used their suggestions like kiwis, plumber, and giraffe to tell the story of a pig named Nia who gets diagnosed with Type 1 Diabetes by Dr. Cockroach. While Nia is initially upset by the news, Dr. Cockroach lets her know that Type 1 Diabetes isn’t her fault, she was born with it, and there are many ways she can mitigate the symptoms to live a happy and healthy life.

Ava and Alexis’ channel: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC2pHEbYWB86O40_1o0S3bNQ

title: How to Lower Your Blood Sugar Immediately | 7 Proven Strategies | Mastering Diabetes
description:
[Dr. Cyrus Khambatta has since decided to leave Mastering Diabetes and is no longer with the company. We wish him all the best.]

The Mastering Diabetes team is back with more invaluable pieces of information to help you thrive on plant-based nutrition. It doesn't matter what form of diabetes you're living with. But when transitioning to a low fat, whole food, plant-based diet, doing it right makes a lot different. The satisfying results will totally change the way you look at your life with the condition. What if this growing community tells you that whoever says life is unbearable with diabetes has the wrong doctors?

On this episode, Robby and Cyrus share 7 strategies proven to lower your blood sugar immediately and naturally. High blood glucose, often called high blood sugar or hyperglycemia, can happen to anyone. Whether you're living with diabetes or not it can get to you. Because when your insulin sensitivity gets interrupted by stress, overeating, a sedentary lifestyle, and a constant fight with a cold or flu -- it's just a matter of time before you find yourself feeling the symptoms. Symptoms that in some instances need an emergency call to your doctor. But how would you know that?

Here's Robby and Cyrus to equip you with facts to help you make better decisions and reclaim your metabolic health.

What to find out:

- How to identify a blood sugar spike
- Some warning signs if your high blood sugar/hyperglycemia needs immediate, emergency intervention
- 7 strategies proven to lower your blood sugar immediately and naturally

Watch and Enjoy!

title: Diabetes Besties Intro – how to manage type 1 diabetes in children
description:
Meet the Diabetes Besties! Betty and her friends show how to manage type 1 diabetes while going to school, playing and having fun. Children living with type 1 diabetes and their families are faced with lots of challenges but with the right care and education diabetes does not stand in the way of a child living their full potential.

If you're a parent, caregiver or health care professional, use the Diabetes Besties animation series to teach children with type 1 diabetes about:

1. Why do I need insulin? (https://youtu.be/62HFICfpEek)

2. How to inject insulin with a syringe. (https://youtu.be/ojcfrT_vphU)

3. How much insulin do I take? (https://youtu.be/QhQHQkSGkho)

4. Low blood sugar and how to recognise it. (https://youtu.be/ibOCNABeGMM)

Download the full Diabetes Besties series and supporting training material for free here - https://www.diabeteswhatsnext.com/global/en/diabetes-besties.html

Established in 2009, Changing Diabetes® in Children is a public-private partnership providing comprehensive care for children and young people living with type 1 diabetes in low- and middle-income countries. This includes free life-saving medicine and supplies for persons up to 25 years of age. The programme has reached over 34,000 vulnerable children and youth to date, runs across 25 partner countries worldwide and aims to reach 100,000 children and youth by 2030.

title: Reversing Type 2 Diabetes | Jason Fung
description:
Reversing Type 2 Diabetes | Jason Fung

"Unlock the Power of Reversing Type 2 Diabetes! 🩸💪
Discover the science behind Type 2 Diabetes and its remarkable reversibility. Join us in this in-depth exploration of glucose tolerance tests compared to insulin levels and learn how fasting can be a game-changer in the battle against insulin resistance.

🔍 Dive into the intricacies of glycolysis and understand the fascinating process of cholesterol transport within your body. We'll unravel the mysteries of hyperinsulinemia and how it causes malfunction in blood sugar regulation.

But it doesn't stop there – we'll show you how to treat the root cause of this condition and prevent pancreatic burnout. Yes, it's true, Type 2 Diabetes is reversible!

🕒 Don't wait! Watch this video to take control of your health and embrace a brighter, diabetes-free future. 🌟

title: Diabetes symptoms | Signs of all types of diabetes | Diabetes UK
description:
What are the symptoms of diabetes? With one in 15 of us now living with condition in the UK, it’s more important than ever to know the signs and symptoms to look out for.

We hope you enjoy this short animation to raise awareness and get more people talking about the signs and symptoms of type 1 and type 2 diabetes.

Common symptoms of diabetes include:

- Going to the toilet a lot, especially at night.
- Being really thirsty.
- Feeling more tired than usual.
- Losing weight without trying to.
- Genital itching or thrush.
- Cuts and wounds take longer to heal.
- Blurred eyesight.

For more information, go to our website: https://www.diabetes.org.uk/diabetes-the-basics/diabetes-symptoms 

------------------------------------------------------

We are Diabetes UK. Our vision is a world where diabetes can do no harm.

We're leading the fight against the UK's biggest and growing health crisis. And it's a fight that involves us all – sharing knowledge and taking on diabetes together.

title: Putting Type 2 Diabetes into REMISSION By Doing This 👉 #diabetes
How To Reverse Diabetes At Home
description:
Book a FREE diet consultation with our Certified Diabetes Reversal Coach visit
https://appointment.diabexy.com/#/customer/free-diabetes-consultation

How To Reverse Diabetes At Home

The biggest rule of Diabetes Reversal is that we have to keep the Glycemic Load below 25 throughout the day. Let us see how we arrive at this figure of 25. 1 Glycemic Load is equal to 20 units of blood sugar, then 25 units of Glycemic Load will give equal to 500 units of blood sugar. 1 unit of insulin covers 50 units of blood sugar. So if our pancreas is working even a little, it will produce 10 units of insulin in a whole day. This means that if we keep our Glycemic Load below 25 for the whole day, then our body will manage the blood sugar for the whole day on its own because it is producing this much insulin on its own.

But in many cases, medical advice will also be required and in many places, dose adjustments will also be required, for that call the number given by Diabexy 8448952009 or book your Free Diabetes Diet Consultation by clicking on the link given above.

title: 6 Foods That Are Excellent For Diabetes
description:
Keeping diabetes under control is critical to living a long and healthy life and this is where diet comes in 🥗
The right diet plays an extremely important role in keeping blood sugar levels under control and ensuring that diabetics can lead a normal life ✅

Opt for foods that are low in fat and calories and high in fibre and proteins. The fibre slows the release of glucose into the bloodstream, preventing blood sugar spikes that worsen blood sugar control and make you feel hungry 😉

Diabetics must include a lot of low glycemic index foods – those that lead to a steady rise in the level of glucose in the blood and a gentle rise in insulin – in their diet 💯

Here are 6 foods that are excellent for diabetes:
#1 Fenugreek Seeds
#2 Insulin Leaves
#3 Cinnamon
#4 Apple Cider Vinegar
#5 Flax Seeds
#6 Chia Seeds

Which among these will you be using? Tell us in the comments 💬

#ItsTimeToHealthify #DiabetesCare #DiabetesFood #FoodForDiabetes #HealthyFood #Tyle2Diabetes #Health #Nutrition #Fibre

title: Gestational Diabetes: Everything You Need to Know
description:
.

Chapters

0:09 Introduction
1:07 Causes of Gestational Diabetes
2:49 Diagnosis and treatment
4:07 Treatment




Gestational diabetes is a condition in which a woman without diabetes develops high blood sugar levels during pregnancy.[2] Gestational diabetes generally results in few symptoms;[2] however, it does increase the risk of pre-eclampsia, depression, and requiring a Caesarean section.[2] Babies born to mothers with poorly treated gestational diabetes are at increased risk of being too large, having low blood sugar after birth, and jaundice.[2] If untreated, it can also result in a stillbirth.[2] Long term, children are at higher risk of being overweight and developing type 2 diabetes.[2]

Gestational diabetes can occur during pregnancy because of insulin resistance or reduced production of insulin.[2] Risk factors include being overweight, previously having gestational diabetes, a family history of type 2 diabetes, and having polycystic ovarian syndrome.[2] Diagnosis is by blood tests.[2] For those at normal risk, screening is recommended between 24 and 28 weeks' gestation.[2][3] For those at high risk, testing may occur at the first prenatal visit.[2]

Prevention is by maintaining a healthy weight and exercising before pregnancy.[2] Gestational diabetes is treated with a diabetic diet, exercise, medication (such as metformin), and possibly insulin injections.[2] Most women are able to manage their blood sugar with diet and exercise.[3] Blood sugar testing among those who are affected is often recommended four times a day.[3] Breastfeeding is recommended as soon as possible after birth.[2]

Gestational diabetes affects 3–9% of pregnancies, depending on the population studied.[3] It is especially common during the last three months of pregnancy.[2] It affects 1% of those under the age of 20 and 13% of those over the age of 44.[3] A number of ethnic groups including Asians, American Indians, Indigenous Australians, and Pacific Islanders are at higher risk.[3][2] In 90% of cases, gestational diabetes will resolve after the baby is born.[2] Women, however, are at an increased risk of developing type 2 diabetes.[3]

title: Reverse Pre-Diabetes Now! Simple Steps to Take (Life-Changing)"
description:
Reverse Pre-Diabetes Now! Simple Steps to Take (Life-Changing)"
#diabetespublichealthresource#centersfordiseasecontrolandprevention#homeopathy#diabetesmanagement#treatment#prediabetes#hba1ctest#alternativemedicines#howtoreverseprediabetes#reverseprediabetes#diabetes#howtoreversepre#pre-diabetes #healthyliving #diabetesawareness #reversingprediabetes #wellness #lifestylechanges #healthtips #diabetesprevention #healthylifestylediabetes#howtopreventdiabetes#howtoreversediabetes#howtoreversetype2diabetes#howmuchexercisetoreverseprediabetes

In this video, learn how to reverse pre-diabetes with simple steps that can change your life. Pre-diabetes is a condition where blood sugar levels are higher than normal but not yet high enough to be diagnosed as type 2 diabetes. By making some key lifestyle changes, you can prevent or even reverse pre-diabetes. 

In this informative and empowering video, we will discuss the importance of a healthy diet, regular exercise, stress management, and other lifestyle factors that can help you take control of your health and reverse pre-diabetes. With the right knowledge and commitment, you can turn the tide on this condition and improve your overall well-being. 

Don't wait until it's too late – start taking action now to reverse pre-diabetes and live a healthier, happier life. Watch this video for practical tips and motivation to make the changes you need to thrive. 

Subscribe to our channel for more valuable insights and tips on how to take charge of your health and well-being. Together, we can reverse pre-diabetes and create a brighter, healthier future for ourselves and our loved ones.

title: Nutritionist Cooks Diabetes Friendly Recipes
description:
Cooking meals when you have diabetes can be challenging, but it’s not impossible! Cooking and eating balanced meals is highly recommended to manage your blood sugar. Nutritionist Jo Sebastian give us a rundown of the types of diabetes, what you can be doing differently once you’re diagnosed, and shares two simple recipes for your next meal prep session at home.

Munggo
Ingredients
3 tbsp oil
2 pc tilapia
3 cloves garlic, minced
1 pc red onions, sliced
2 pc tomatoes, sliced
1 cup munggo beans
1 cup malunggay leaves

Procedure:
In a pan with hot oil, cook the fish for 2-3 minutes on each side. Set aside and shred.
Using the same pan, saute garlic and onions followed by the tomatoes.
Add your munggo beans and fill with 4 cups of water. Bring to a boil and simmer for about 40 minutes or until the beans are mashable. Add your malunggay and cook for an additional 2 minutes.

Makes 6 servings

Egg Wrap
Ingredients:
3 pc eggs
1 pc cucumbers, cut in half and sliced
3 pcs tomatoes, sliced
kesong puti
Greek-style yogurt

Procedure:
Beat all eggs in a bowl. On a non-stick skillet, add oil and eggs.
Once the egg is set, loosen the sides and flip. Cook for an additional 30 seconds and set aside.
Assemble by placing the veggies and cheese at the bottom of the egg. Drizzle with yogurt and roll into a wrap.

title: 12 Unusual Symptoms of Diabetes | Diabetes Symptoms | Diabetes Mellitus |  | Diabetes Warning Signs
description:
12 Unusual Symptoms of Diabetes | Diabetes Symptoms | Diabetes Mellitus |  | Diabetes Warning Signs

In this video we have discussed some early and unusual signs and symptoms of diabetes.
There are mainly 3 types of diabetes:
Type – I: where insulin is completely absent
Type – II: where either insulin formation decreases, or the body is not responding to the insulin formed.
Gestational diabetes - Gestational diabetes develops in pregnant women who have never had diabetes

How can you tell if you have diabetes? 
Most early symptoms are from higher-than-normal levels of glucose.

What are the early signs of Diabetes?

Hunger and Fatigue
The food we eat is converted into glucose, which our cells use for energy. Our cells need insulin to take in glucose. But in diabetes there isn’t enough or any insulin. So the glucose can't get into them and you have no energy. This can make you hungrier and more tired than usual.

Peeing more often and being thirstier
A person with diabetes may need to pee a lot more, than an average person.

Dry mouth & Itchy Skin
Because the body is using a lot of fluid to make urine, there's less moisture available for other things. This can make you dehydrated, and your Mouth may feel dry. 

Blurred vision
Changing fluid levels in body can make the lens in Eyes swell up. 

What are the symptoms of Diabetes
These appear after the glucose level is high for a long time.

Fungal or yeast infections:
Excess glucose in your body promotes fungal infection. In diabetes there can be fungal infections
Between fingers and toes
Under breasts
In or around genital organs.

Slow-healing sores or cuts
Over time, high blood sugar can affect the blood flow and it may even cause nerve damage. 
That makes it hard for your body to heal wounds.

Pain or numbness in feet or legs
This is also a result of nerve damage caused by high blood glucose.

Unplanned weight loss
If your body can't get energy from the food you consume, then it will start burning muscle and fat for energy.  This leads to the unintentional weight loss.

Nausea & Vomiting
When the body burns fat, it makes ketones. These ketones can build up in blood to dangerous levels. These Ketones can make you feel sick.

What are the warning Signs of Diabetes Complications
Signs of diabetes' complications may include:
Slow-healing sores or cuts
Itchy skin (usually around the vaginal or groin area)
Frequent yeast infections.
Recent weight gain.
Velvety, dark skin changes of the neck, armpit, and groin, called Acanthosis nigricans
Numbness and tingling of the hands and feet
Decreased vision
Impotence or erectile dysfunction (ED)

A serious complication of prolonged increase in blood glucose is Diabetic Coma
This serious complication can even lead to death. 
It may occur with either type of diabetes, though it’s more common in type 2. 

Other complications of diabetes are:
Heart disease and stroke
Diabetic retinopathy
Glaucoma
Cataract
Chronic kidney disease (CKD)
Neuropathy
Gum diseases

When to visit a Doctor
If you're older than 45 or have other risks for diabetes, it's important to get tested. 
When you diagnose the condition early, you can avoid nerve damage, Heart trouble, and other complications.

title: Oatmeal Diet Put to the Test for Diabetes Treatment
description:
Oatmeal for diabetes treatment. What are the extraordinary, lasting benefits we may get from a few days of an oatmeal diet?

This is the third and final video in this series on oatmeal for diabetes treatment. If you missed the others, see Is Oatmeal Good for People with Diabetes? (https://nutritionfacts.org/video/is-oatmeal-good-for-people-with-diabetes) and How Does Oatmeal Help with Blood Sugars? (https://nutritionfacts.org/video/how-does-oatmeal-help-with-blood-sugars).

If you are on insulin or blood sugar pills and want to give the oatmeal diet a try, make sure to talk with your medical professional first so your medications can be closely monitored and adjusted as needed.

What else might oatmeal do? See:
• Flashback Friday: Can Oatmeal Reverse Heart Disease? (https://nutritionfacts.org/video/flashback-friday-can-oatmeal-reverse-heart-disease/)
• Can Oatmeal Help Fatty Liver Disease? (https://nutritionfacts.org/video/can-oatmeal-help-fatty-liver-disease/)
• Flashback Friday: Which Is a Better Breakfast—Cereal or Oatmeal? (https://nutritionfacts.org/video/flashback-friday-which-is-a-better-breakfast-cereal-or-oatmeal/)

New subscribers to our e-newsletter always receive a free gift. Get yours here: https://nutritionfacts.org/subscribe/.

Have a question about this video? Leave it in the comment section at http://nutritionfacts.org/video/oatmeal-diet-put-to-the-test-for-diabetes-treatment and someone on the NutritionFacts.org team will try to answer it.


Want to get a list of links to all the scientific sources used in this video? Click on Sources Cited at https://nutritionfacts.org/video/oatmeal-diet-put-to-the-test-for-diabetes-treatment. You’ll also find a transcript and acknowledgements for the video, my blog and speaking tour schedule, and an easy way to search (by translated language even) through our videos spanning more than 2,000 health topics. 

Thanks for watching. I hope you’ll join in the evidence-based nutrition revolution!
-Michael Greger, MD FACLM

title: How to Lower Your Blood Sugar Immediately | 7 Proven Strategies | Mastering Diabetes
description:
[Dr. Cyrus Khambatta has since decided to leave Mastering Diabetes and is no longer with the company. We wish him all the best.]

The Mastering Diabetes team is back with more invaluable pieces of information to help you thrive on plant-based nutrition. It doesn't matter what form of diabetes you're living with. But when transitioning to a low fat, whole food, plant-based diet, doing it right makes a lot different. The satisfying results will totally change the way you look at your life with the condition. What if this growing community tells you that whoever says life is unbearable with diabetes has the wrong doctors?

On this episode, Robby and Cyrus share 7 strategies proven to lower your blood sugar immediately and naturally. High blood glucose, often called high blood sugar or hyperglycemia, can happen to anyone. Whether you're living with diabetes or not it can get to you. Because when your insulin sensitivity gets interrupted by stress, overeating, a sedentary lifestyle, and a constant fight with a cold or flu -- it's just a matter of time before you find yourself feeling the symptoms. Symptoms that in some instances need an emergency call to your doctor. But how would you know that?

Here's Robby and Cyrus to equip you with facts to help you make better decisions and reclaim your metabolic health.

What to find out:

- How to identify a blood sugar spike
- Some warning signs if your high blood sugar/hyperglycemia needs immediate, emergency intervention
- 7 strategies proven to lower your blood sugar immediately and naturally

Watch and Enjoy!

title: Top 7 Foods for Diabetics
description:
Top 7 Foods for Diabetics
Diabetes is a condition in which the body is unable to properly use and store glucose (a type of sugar). To manage diabetes, it is important to eat a healthy and balanced diet that is low in sugar and saturated fats, and high in fiber.

Some specific foods that are recommended for people with diabetes include:

Fruits and vegetables
Whole grains (such as whole wheat bread, oatmeal, and brown rice)
Lean proteins (such as chicken, fish, and beans)
Healthy fats (such as olive oil, nuts, and avocado)
It is also important for people with diabetes to monitor their carbohydrate intake, as carbohydrates can have a big impact on blood sugar levels.

It's always a good idea to consult a dietitian or healthcare professional for personalized advice on maintaining a healthy diet.

How to Reduce Fasting Blood Sugar | Diabexy Talk Show on Diabetes Ep 02

🙏🏻 We are thankful to our subscribers who regularly watch and comment on our videos. After watching the videos, people generally have many quires which they put in the comment box regarding diabetes. This program is designed to answer those quires. Therefore, Diabexy brings you the show "Diabexy Talk Show on Diabetes" Here, we will answer your questions precisely, debunk many diabetes-related myths, and raise awareness about the condition. You may please ask your questions by clicking on the above link and submitting your queries.  

🤔 Why does fasting blood sugar decrease slowly? 

The reason fasting blood sugar decreases gradually is that blood sugar levels do not remain constant in the body. Our body follows a circadian rhythm, and this internal clock resets around 4 AM. This reset allows the pancreas to capture the circulating insulin and clear the blood sugar. As the liver pulls in this insulin, the pancreas starts pumping fresh insulin. This process doesn't have a significant impact on blood sugar levels. However, in diabetic patients, the pancreas is weakened. While the liver continues its function of pulling in the excess insulin, the pancreas fails to release fresh insulin. This leads to a decrease in insulin levels in the body, while the level of glucagon, which is insulin's opposite, increases. Glucagon then converts the fat stored in the liver into sugar, releasing new glucose into the bloodstream and causing blood sugar levels to rise.

title: Abbott Freestyle Libre 3 Review — A Discrete CGM For Diabetics
description:
Abbott Laboratories newest continuous glucose monitor is now available at participating retail pharmacies and through durable medical equipment suppliers. The Freestyle Libre 3 was approved by the FDA in June and is a step up from previous Abbott systems. 

Abbott Laboratories and Dexcom are the leaders in the CGM market, which hit $5.1 billion in revenue in 2021 and is expected to reach $13.2 billion by 2028, according to Vantage Market Research. Abbott’s CGM systems, called FreeStyle Libre, generated $3.7 billion in revenue last year, with 4 million users globally.

CNBC's Erin Black, a type 1 diabetic, tested out the Libre 3 for over a month. Here is her review.

title: Diabetes symptoms in men, women in Hindi II Diabetes ke lakshan kya hote hain II Madhumeh ke lakshan
description:
Know about diabetes symptoms in Hindi. 

In this video, you will know about early symptoms of diabetes (diabetes ke shuruati lakshan kya hote hai)

Diabetes is a chronic condition and early identification of diabetes-related symptoms can slow down the progress of diabetes-related complications.

If a person develops any of the diabetes-related symptoms as discussed in the video he/she should consult a doctor and get evaluated.

Following are the early diabetes symptoms in men and women (Diabetes ke lakshan kya hote hain?) :

1. One of the early diabetes symptoms in men and women, is increased hunger and appetite (also called Polyphagia) associated with fatigue.
2. Also, a patient can develop an increased frequency of urination if diabetes develops ( (also called Polydipsia).
3. Increased urination is a diabetes symptom that leads to other problems like dehydration, increased thirst ( (also called Polydipsia), and dry skin.
4. A diabetic patient has an increased risk of infection especially urinary tract infection and skin infection.
5. Other diabetes symptoms in men and women are blurring of vision, tingling, and numbness of feet and hands.
6. A diabetic patient may also develop severe symptoms like pain abdomen, nausea, and vomiting.

Watch the whole video and subscribe to our channel to get authentic medical information.

Sources: 
Diabetes: Journal of American Diabetic Association
The United States National Library of Medicine

Website: https://www.thydoc.com/
Health blogs: https://www.thydoc.com/health-blog/

title: How Diabetes develops (what happens in diabetes) #diabetes
description:
Do you know how diabetes develops? Let me explain. Diabetes is a condition where the glucose level in our blood starts to rise. Normally, a hormone called insulin, produced by the pancreas, helps regulate blood sugar. Insulin converts extra glucose in the blood into glycogen, keeping sugar levels balanced.
But in some people, the pancreas doesn’t produce enough insulin, or the body stops using it effectively. This causes glucose to build up in the blood instead of being converted. Over time, this condition leads to diabetes.
If left untreated, high blood sugar can cause serious health issues like nerve damage, kidney problems, and heart disease. That’s why early diagnosis is so crucial. Stay aware and take care
#diabetesawareness #shorts

title: Complete Guide to Type 2 Diabetes Remission | The Diabetes Code Clinic (Part 1/6)
description:
Are you struggling with Type 2 diabetes or know someone who is? In this first session of The Diabetes Code Clinic, renowned expert Dr. Jason Fung takes you on a comprehensive journey to understand Type 2 diabetes and the steps you can take to reverse it effectively.

Dr. Fung breaks down the science, myths, and proven methods behind reversing Type 2 diabetes naturally without relying solely on medications. Whether you're new to managing diabetes or looking for advanced insights, this video is your guide to a healthier future.

This is Part 1 of a 6 part series that is used in Dr. Fung's Toronto based diabetes remission clinic.

▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬  
Timestamps for Easy Navigation:
⏱ 00:00 – Welcome to the Diabetes Code Clinic
⏱ 00:30 – Understanding Type 2 Diabetes
⏱ 03:10 – Type 1 vs. Type 2 Diabetes: Key Differences
⏱ 04:00 – How Excess Sugar Damages Your Body
⏱ 06:30 – How to Reverse Type 2 Diabetes Naturally
⏱ 09:00 – Actionable Steps to Start Today
⏱ 11:15 – Key Takeaways for Diabetes Reversal
▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬  

What You'll Learn in This Video:
🔹 What is Type 2 Diabetes?
Understand the root cause of Type 2 diabetes and how it differs from Type 1. Learn why it’s considered a reversible dietary condition.

🔹 The Hidden Dangers of Sugar Overload
Discover how excess sugar impacts your body, especially the liver and pancreas, and leads to insulin resistance.

🔹  Steps to Reverse Type 2 Diabetes
Dr. Fung explains simple yet powerful strategies, including:

Adopting a low-carbohydrate diet to reduce sugar intake.
Practicing intermittent fasting to give your body a break from constant insulin spikes.
Tracking your progress with a food journal and glucose monitor for better results.

🔹  Why Medications Alone Aren’t the Answer
Learn why traditional treatments often fail to address the root cause and how dietary changes can achieve long-term remission.

Who Should Watch This Video?
This video is perfect for:

Anyone diagnosed with Type 2 diabetes seeking a non-invasive solution.
Caregivers, family members, and friends supporting loved ones with diabetes.
Individuals interested in preventing diabetes or improving overall health.

Key Takeaways:
1️⃣ Type 2 diabetes is a reversible condition when treated as a dietary disease rather than a chronic illness.
2️⃣ It’s caused by the accumulation of sugar in your body over time.
3️⃣ Sustainable lifestyle changes, like reducing carbohydrate intake and intermittent fasting, can help “empty the sugar bowl” and restore insulin sensitivity.
4️⃣ Consistency and tracking are vital – small, consistent steps lead to big changes.

Why Trust Dr. Jason Fung?
Dr. Jason Fung is a world-renowned nephrologist, diabetes educator, and the author of best-selling books The Diabetes Code and The Obesity Code. With years of experience helping patients reverse diabetes, Dr. Fung is dedicated to empowering people with the knowledge and tools to regain control of their health.

📢 Share Your Journey:
Have you tried reversing Type 2 diabetes? Let us know your experience in the comments below. Dr. Fung and the community would love to hear from you!

title: Why Should You Use Apple Cider Vinegar (ACV) for Diabetes?
description:
What does apple cider vinegar have to do with diabetes? You’ll be surprised how this simple product can benefit in controlling your blood sugar levels. But it’s not all roses: ACV can also have some side effects that you truly wouldn’t want. So let’s talk about all of this today!

🚨Take a 60-second quiz to evaluate your diabetes health state👉https://bit.ly/3D9D1gm
________________________________________________

Apple cider vinegar is made in a two-step fermentation process. Firstly, apples are crushed and combined with yeast to convert sugar into alcohol. Second, bacteria are added to ferment the alcohol into acetic acid. And specifically acetic acid in ACV may contribute to health benefits. It does that in several ways:

- Lowers blood sugar levels;
- Decreases insulin levels;
- Boosts metabolism;
- Increases fat burning;
- Reduces appetite.

When you have diabetes, you have a lot riding on your blood sugar levels. You know that diabetes management is mainly based on physical activity and a healthy diet, but surprisingly, apple cider vinegar can also be included in reducing blood sugar levels. One study that was published in the Diabetes Care journal looked into how ACV can help manage blood sugar levels in type 2 diabetes. It showed that consuming 2 tablespoons of apple cider vinegar before bedtime reduces fasting blood sugar by 4% the following morning. Another research shows that consuming vinegar after a high-carb meal can improve insulin sensitivity by as much as 19–34% and significantly reduce blood sugar levels.

Another important benefit of ACV for diabetes is weight loss. Being obese is a risk factor for diabetes. Of course, the best way to lose weight is by exercising and investing in a healthy diet. But there are other means you can consider when looking to lose weight. And some studies link apple cider vinegar with weight loss. One research showed that consuming apple cider vinegar can result in effective weight loss during a 12-week period. In this study, people took 1–2 tablespoons of apple cider vinegar diluted in water every day. And they lost an average of 4lbs each, as well as visceral body fat!

How should you use apple cider vinegar?
You can use it with olive oil as a salad dressing. It’s especially tasty with leafy greens, cucumbers, and tomatoes. But the easiest way is to mix it into water. Just add 1–2 tablespoons of apple cider vinegar into a glass of water, stir it up and drink it. Remember, you don’t want to take any shots of apple cider vinegar straight from the bottle! Because of vinegar’s acidic compounds, it should always be mixed. Otherwise, you risk side effects like tooth enamel erosion or drug interactions.

title: Early 5 signs and symptoms of diabetes #diabetes
description:
Let’s talk about the 5 early symptoms of diabetes that you should never ignore:
1. Increased Thirst – High blood sugar dehydrates your body, making you feel constantly thirsty.
2. Frequent Urination – Excess glucose forces your kidneys to work harder, leading to frequent trips to the bathroom, especially at night.
3. Fatigue – When your body can’t use glucose for energy, you feel tired all the time.
4. Increased Hunger – Your body demands more food to compensate for its lack of energy, even if you’ve eaten enough.
5. Unexplained Weight Loss – The body starts breaking down fat and muscle for energy, causing noticeable weight loss.

title: Is Diabetes Permanently Curable? | Diabexy
description:

Is Diabetes Permanently Curable?

In today's video, we will discuss whether diabetes is permanently curable or not? 

Diabetes is caused by glucose intolerance, so which means that if the glucose in your body stays within a particular level, your blood sugar will not rise. However, if it goes above this level, the blood sugar level will increase. Therefore, the term 'permanent cure' is not accurate for diabetes. The correct terms are diabetes reversal or diabetes remission. If your blood sugar is below a certain level, you can lead a normal life. However, if your glucose load exceeds your handling capacity, it means your blood sugar level will rise, leading to diabetes. Hence, the term 'permanent cure of diabetes' is not accurate. If you want more information on this topic, please type it in full in the comments below.

description:
Book a FREE diet consultation with our Certified Diabetes Reversal Coach visit
https://appointment.diabexy.com/#/customer/free-diabetes-consultation

12 Hours Fasting For Diabetes Reversal

The big rule of Diabetes Reversal is that we have to keep a fasting window of 12 hours and an Eating window of 12 hours. A fasting window of 12 hours is very important, Suppose we have breakfast at 8 in the morning then we will definitely have dinner by 8 in the evening. This is our fasting window from 8 pm to 8 am. Whenever we eat something, digestion is required. Digestion requires a lot of metabolic energy. The metabolic energy we save from digestion at night is used by our body for healing, repair, and growth. If we give a fasting window of 12 hours, then when we are sleeping at night, our body is working rapidly for healing, repair and growth development.

title: Signs and symptoms of diabetes, explained | JUST THE FAQs
description:
Over 37 million people in the U.S. have diabetes and that number is climbing. Here are the signs and symptoms.

RELATED: This new diabetes drug could be a game changer for obesity https://bit.ly/3WRyzck

Diabetes is expected to surge dramatically in young people over the next several decades in what experts say should be a wake-up call to prioritize health in America.

Researchers estimate that in 2060, there could be as many as 220,000 people under age 20 with Type 2 diabetes, an increase of nearly 700%, according to a Centers for Disease Control and Prevention study released Thursday. Those with Type 1, the most common type in young people, could see a 65% increase.

Overall, the findings estimate that more than half a million young people could have diabetes in 2060, if current rates continue and with population increase. In 2017, that number was 213,000, according to the study published in American Diabetes Association's Diabetes Care.

Diabetes is expected to surge dramatically in young people over the next several decades in what experts say should be a wake-up call to prioritize health in America.

Researchers estimate that in 2060, there could be as many as 220,000 people under age 20 with Type 2 diabetes, an increase of nearly 700%, according to a Centers for Disease Control and Prevention study released Thursday. Those with Type 1, the most common type in young people, could see a 65% increase.

Overall, the findings estimate that more than half a million young people could have diabetes in 2060, if current rates continue and with population increase. In 2017, that number was 213,000, according to the study published in American Diabetes Association's Diabetes Care.

title: The Connection Between Diabetes and Carbohydrates
DIABETES DIET CHART ಮಧುಮೇಹ ಆಹಾರ ಕ್ರಮ
description:
#BACKPAIN #DRJITHESHNAMBIAR #SCIATICA  #DIETCHART #PHYSIOTHERAPY #NATUROPATHY #AYURVEDA #NATURECURE #NISARGACHIKITSALAYA #SIRSI #EXERCISE #FOOD #FASTING #DIABETES #DIET

DIABETES DIET CHART ಮಧುಮೇಹ ಆಹಾರ ಕ್ರಮ

Designing a diabetes diet chart involves a systematic process that aims to regulate blood sugar levels, manage weight, and promote overall health for individuals with diabetes. This process takes into account various factors to create a balanced and sustainable eating plan tailored to each person's unique needs and preferences.

Assessment: The first step involves assessing the individual's medical history, current health status, activity levels, and any specific dietary restrictions. Understanding their goals and challenges related to diabetes management is essential to create an effective and personalized diet chart.

Caloric Intake: Determining an appropriate caloric intake is crucial. The number of calories consumed plays a role in maintaining a healthy weight and managing blood sugar levels. For instance, a 1500 to 1800 kcal range might be chosen as a starting point, but this can vary based on factors such as age, gender, activity level, and weight loss goals.

Carbohydrate Management: Carbohydrates significantly impact blood sugar levels. The diet chart focuses on selecting complex carbohydrates, which are broken down slowly, leading to gradual increases in blood sugar. Portion control is emphasized to manage carbohydrate intake and prevent spikes in glucose levels.

Glycemic Index: Foods are classified based on their glycemic index (GI), indicating how quickly they raise blood sugar levels. Foods with lower GI values are prioritized, as they have a gentler impact on blood glucose levels. This helps in maintaining stability and avoiding sudden fluctuations.

Balanced Nutrient Profile: The diet chart ensures a balanced distribution of macronutrients – carbohydrates, proteins, and fats. Proteins provide satiety and prevent muscle loss, while healthy fats offer sustained energy. The right balance supports overall health and blood sugar management.

Fiber Inclusion: High-fiber foods like whole grains, legumes, vegetables, and fruits are integral to the diet. Fiber slows down digestion, preventing rapid spikes in blood sugar levels and promoting digestive health. Adequate fiber intake is crucial for long-term diabetes management.

Lean Proteins: Incorporating lean sources of protein supports muscle maintenance and helps regulate blood sugar levels. Lean meats, poultry, fish, eggs, dairy products, and plant-based protein sources like tofu and legumes are chosen to provide a variety of options.

Portion Control: Controlling portion sizes helps in managing calorie intake and preventing overeating. Specific serving sizes of different foods are recommended to avoid consuming excess calories and carbohydrates.

Meal Timing: Consistent meal timing is advised to maintain stable blood sugar levels throughout the day. Spacing out meals and snacks helps prevent sudden drops or spikes in glucose levels.

Hydration: Staying hydrated is essential for overall health and metabolism. However, beverages containing added sugars are limited due to their potential to raise blood sugar levels quickly.

Monitoring and Adjustment: Regular monitoring of blood sugar levels is important to assess the effectiveness of the diet chart. Based on the results, adjustments can be made to caloric intake, carbohydrate distribution, and other dietary factors.

Individualization: Every individual's response to food and diabetes management is unique. The diet chart is customized based on personal preferences, cultural considerations, and health goals. Consulting a registered dietitian or healthcare professional ensures that the plan is tailored to the individual's needs.

In conclusion, designing a diabetes diet chart involves a thoughtful and individualized approach. It aims to strike a balance between managing blood sugar levels, promoting overall health, and meeting personal preferences. By considering factors such as caloric intake, carbohydrate quality, portion control, and regular monitoring, individuals with diabetes can create a sustainable eating plan that supports their well-being and long-term diabetes management.

This new PSA campaign aims to raise awareness of prediabetes, a serious health condition that affects more than 1 in 3 American adults and often leads to type 2 diabetes. Nearly 90% of people with prediabetes don’t know they have it and aren’t aware of the long-term risks to their health. To help those with prediabetes know where they stand and prevent type 2 diabetes, the American Medical Association (AMA), and the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) have partnered with the Ad Council to launch the first national PSA campaign about prediabetes. The PSAs encourage viewers to visit DoIHavePrediabetes.org where they can take a one-minute risk test to know where they stand and to discover how they can decrease their risk of developing type 2 diabetes.

title: Top 7 Foods for Diabetics
description:
Top 7 Foods for Diabetics
Diabetes is a condition in which the body is unable to properly use and store glucose (a type of sugar). To manage diabetes, it is important to eat a healthy and balanced diet that is low in sugar and saturated fats, and high in fiber.

Some specific foods that are recommended for people with diabetes include:

Fruits and vegetables
Whole grains (such as whole wheat bread, oatmeal, and brown rice)
Lean proteins (such as chicken, fish, and beans)
Healthy fats (such as olive oil, nuts, and avocado)
It is also important for people with diabetes to monitor their carbohydrate intake, as carbohydrates can have a big impact on blood sugar levels.

It's always a good idea to consult a dietitian or healthcare professional for personalized advice on maintaining a healthy diet.

How to Reduce Fasting Blood Sugar | Diabexy Talk Show on Diabetes Ep 02

🙏🏻 We are thankful to our subscribers who regularly watch and comment on our videos. After watching the videos, people generally have many quires which they put in the comment box regarding diabetes. This program is designed to answer those quires. Therefore, Diabexy brings you the show "Diabexy Talk Show on Diabetes" Here, we will answer your questions precisely, debunk many diabetes-related myths, and raise awareness about the condition. You may please ask your questions by clicking on the above link and submitting your queries.  

🤔 Why does fasting blood sugar decrease slowly? 

The reason fasting blood sugar decreases gradually is that blood sugar levels do not remain constant in the body. Our body follows a circadian rhythm, and this internal clock resets around 4 AM. This reset allows the pancreas to capture the circulating insulin and clear the blood sugar. As the liver pulls in this insulin, the pancreas starts pumping fresh insulin. This process doesn't have a significant impact on blood sugar levels. However, in diabetic patients, the pancreas is weakened. While the liver continues its function of pulling in the excess insulin, the pancreas fails to release fresh insulin. This leads to a decrease in insulin levels in the body, while the level of glucagon, which is insulin's opposite, increases. Glucagon then converts the fat stored in the liver into sugar, releasing new glucose into the bloodstream and causing blood sugar levels to rise.

title: Frequent Urination and Thirst | Diabetes Symptoms #shorts #shortsvideo #diabetes
description:
In this video, we will discuss the prominent symptoms of #diabetes, focusing on excessive urination and constant thirst. If you find yourself peeing frequently and feeling thirsty all the time, it could be a sign of diabetes. Stay tuned to learn more about these common warning signs and how to manage them. Remember, early detection is key to effectively managing diabetes and maintaining your overall health. Watch now to stay informed about diabetes symptoms

High Blood Sugar Levels: In diabetes, the body either does not produce enough insulin (Type 1 diabetes) or cannot effectively use the insulin it produces (Type 2 diabetes). This leads to elevated blood sugar levels (hyperglycemia). The kidneys attempt to remove the excess glucose from the blood by filtering it out into the urine. This excess glucose in the urine pulls water along with it, leading to increased urine production (polyuria).

Dehydration: Increased urination can result in dehydration, which triggers the sensation of thirst. The body tries to replenish lost fluids by making you feel thirsty.
#Diabetes #FrequentUrination #HighBloodSugar #DiabetesSymptoms #HealthTips

title: Diabetes Remission Vs Diabetes Reversal | Diabexy
description:
✅ What to Eat & Avoid in Diabetes? Download EGL Chart - https://diabexy.com/products/diabexy-egl-chart-download

✅ Book a FREE diet consultation with our Certified Diabetes Reversal Coach ➡ Visit https://appointment.diabexy.com/#/customer/free-diabetes-consultation 

✅ Diabetes Remission Vs Diabetes Reversal 

In this informative video, we delve into the critical differences between diabetes remission and diabetes reversal. Many individuals are often confused about these terms, but understanding them is crucial for managing diabetes effectively.

Diabetes remission refers to a state where blood sugar levels are controlled without the need for medications or insulin. This can be achieved through significant lifestyle changes, such as adopting a healthy diet, regular exercise, and weight loss. However, it's important to note that diabetes can still return if these lifestyle changes are not maintained.

On the other hand, diabetes reversal goes beyond remission. It signifies a state where the body's insulin production and sensitivity are restored to a level where diabetes is no longer present. This is often achieved through intensive lifestyle interventions, such as very low-calorie diets or bariatric surgery, which can lead to substantial weight loss and significant improvements in insulin function.

In this video, we'll explore the strategies for both remission and reversal, including the challenges and benefits associated with each approach. Whether you're living with diabetes or supporting someone who is, understanding these concepts can empower you to make informed decisions about managing the condition and improving overall health. 

Diabexy's mission is to eradicate diabetes from India like polio and it is possible with the help of knowledge and discipline that's why Diabexy makes educational videos, Diabexy makes food products that have very low glycemic load and also provides free consultations with medical doctors and dietitians. So if you also believe that diabetes can be eradicated then we can work together to eradicate diabetes from India.

Diabexy products are specially designed for diabetics. They help in managing blood sugar levels while supplementing your daily nutritional requirements. With over a decade of research and development to develop low glycemic load food products, we believe that it is possible to reverse diabetes (डायबिटीज रेवेर्सल) with the help of healthy eating habits and disciplined lifestyle.

At Diabexy, we believe it is possible to reverse diabetes with the support of knowledge and discipline. Let's eradicate Diabetes like polio from India.  We provide a free consultation with our team of certified dieticians and licensed medical doctors to support your Diabetes Reversal Plan.

title: Worst foods for diabetics
description:
#diabetesprevention #healthyfoodchoices #diabetesawareness #healthyliving #diabeticdiet #diabeteseducation #healthytips #sugarfree #lowcarb #balanceddiet #eatwell #diabetesmanagement #healthylifestyle #diabetesrisk #nutritionalguidelines #foodchoices #diabeticfriendly #diabetespreventiontips #healthychoices #knowyourfood

In this informative video, we delve into the topic of "WHICH FOODS INCREASE RISK OF DIABETES." Join us as we discuss the common culprits that contribute to diabetes and learn about the essential steps to decrease your diabetes risk.

Are you concerned about your health and the potential risk of developing diabetes? It's vital to understand how your dietary choices play a significant role in this condition. In our video, "The Diabetic Culprits: Avoid These Foods to Decrease Diabetes Risk," we shed light on the foods you should steer clear of to protect yourself.

Our experts unveil the significant impact that certain food choices can have on raising your diabetes risk. We explore the harmful effects of sugar-laden beverages, processed foods loaded with unhealthy fats, and high-carbohydrate options that spike blood sugar levels. Acquiring knowledge about these diabetic culprits empowers you to make smarter food choices and reduce the likelihood of diabetes.

Furthermore, we provide valuable insights into healthier alternatives to incorporate into your diet. Learning how to make nutritious choices can make a remarkable difference in controlling your diabetes risk. By avoiding these diabetic culprits, you can take a proactive step towards safeguarding your health.

Stay tuned throughout our video to discover the proactive measures you can take to decrease your diabetes risk. From incorporating more fresh produce and fiber-rich foods to understanding portion control, we provide practical tips to make positive changes in your dietary habits.

Now is the time to take charge of your well-being. Watch "The Diabetic Culprits: Avoid These Foods to Decrease Diabetes Risk" to gain a deeper understanding of the foods that increase the risk of diabetes, and start making healthier choices today!

title: 10 Foods Diabetics Should NEVER Eat (Avoid Them At All Costs)!
description:
People with diabetes need to be mindful of their diet because certain foods can significantly impact blood sugar levels. Here are 10 Foods Diabetics Should Never Eat (Avoid Them at All Costs) as much as possible. Here's why:

✔️ Blood Sugar Control: Diabetes disrupts the body's ability to regulate blood sugar (glucose). 

✔️ Insulin: People with type 1 diabetes don't produce enough insulin, a hormone that helps cells absorb glucose for energy.

✔️ Insulin Resistance: In type 2 diabetes, the body becomes less responsive to insulin, meaning glucose can't enter cells effectively.
Food Impact:

✅ Carbohydrates: These break down into glucose, the primary source of energy for the body. Consuming too many carbs can cause a rapid spike in blood sugar.

✅ Sugary Foods: Processed foods, sugary drinks, and sweets are high in added sugars, leading to significant blood sugar fluctuations.

✅ Unhealthy Fats: Saturated and trans fats can increase insulin resistance and contribute to heart disease, a common complication of diabetes.

I hope you learn much from this video and begin to avoid them for better blood sugar management. Get our UNDO DIABETES GUIDE here 👉 https://www.cooperwellnesscenter.com/books.

 #diabetesdiet #healthyeating #undodiabetes #10bestfoods

What Is Diabetes?
Diabetes is a condition that happens either when your pancreas does not produce or manufacture enough insulin or when your body cannot effectively utilize the insulin it stimulates.

Hyperglycemia (increased blood sugar) is the most common effect of uncontrolled diabetes. Over time, it leads to serious damage or disease to many of the organs and systems in your body, especially the nerves and blood vessels.


Diabetes Can Be Of Three Types Depending On Age And Other Factors:

Type 1 diabetes (occurs in infants),
Type 2 diabetes (occurs in adults)
Gestational diabetes (occurs in women at the time of pregnancy).


These are the top 10 warning signs of diabetes:

- Frequent Urination
- Frequent Thirst
- Frequent Hunger
- Unexplained Weight Loss
- Fatigue
- Difficulty In Vision
- Slow-Healing Cuts And Wounds
- Frequent Infection
- Numbness Or Tingling In Your Hands Or Feet
- Discoloration Of Your Skin

Take Away: For Diabetes Patients
---------------------------------------------------------------
- Diabetes is a metabolic disease that occurs when your body does not produce enough insulin or cannot effectively use insulin.
- It is a major cause of mild to severe diseases like blindness, kidney failure, heart attacks, stroke, and lower limb amputation in many populations.
- Diabetes is a treatable disease, and its significance can be prevented or discontinued with diet, physical activity, medication, and regular screening and treatment for severe complications.
- Significant warning signs and symptoms are early detectable, which can be very helpful in the early diagnosis and treatment of diabetes.
- The three main warning signs of diabetes are frequent urination, frequent thirst, and frequent hunger.

Diabetes নিয়ে এতো ঘ্যান- ঘ্যান কেন? । Dr Kunal Sarkar | 5 tips for diabetes patient
description:
If you are a diabetes patient, remember these tips that can help you prevent and control diabetes. #medicalandhealth #medicalhealth #medicalhealthtips #medical_and_health #healthtipsshorts #healthtipsforhappylife  

#1 Take medicines regularly
You should take your diabetes medicines regularly as prescribed by your doctor. This will help to keep your blood sugar levels in control.

#2 Do a regular heart check-up
Diabetes can increase your risk of heart disease. So, you need to do a regular heart checkup.

#3 Have a proper diet
You should have a proper diet and eat healthy foods such as vegetables, fruits, whole grains, and lean protein. Avoid eating sugary and fatty foods.

#4 Walk regularly
Walking is a great exercise for diabetes patients. It helps to lower blood sugar levels and also improves your overall health.

#5 Be physically active
You should be physically active for at least 30 minutes per day. Exercise can help to lower your blood sugar levels.

#6 Monitor your blood sugar levels
You should monitor your blood sugar levels regularly. This will help you to know if your treatment plan is working or not.

#7 Consult your nearest doctor
If you have any concerns about your health, you should consult your nearest doctor. He/she will help you to manage your diabetes in a better way.

These are some tips that can help you in preventing and controlling diabetes.
